title: CLI integration golden tests for root command

finding: Software Factory scripts test suite now executes the real root CLI for help, unknown-command, and operation-list JSON surfaces.

followUp: Expand integration matrix to trigger/doctor and strict failure-path assertions with mocked external tool outputs.

title: Effect-native CLI error and execution hardening
trigger: manual-cli-migration
finding: Root CLI now maps command failures through tagged Effect errors and utility command execution no longer depends on Promise bridge helpers.
evidence: Added factory/errors.ts and switched handlers to runUtilityCommandEffect with Effect.tryPromise mappings; added script guardrails for parseArgs/process.argv/runScript misuse.
followUp: Extract operation/trigger/doctor into typed Effect service modules and remove stringly override maps.

title: Extracted control-plane registry and doctor services
trigger: manual-cli-migration
finding: Root CLI now delegates registry IO/inspection and doctor diagnostics to dedicated factory modules, reducing monolith coupling and clarifying command wiring boundaries.
evidence: Added control-plane-types.ts, control-plane-registry.ts, control-plane-doctor.ts and removed in-file read/list/explain/doctor implementations from software-factory.ts.
followUp: Continue by extracting operation/trigger execution orchestration into a dedicated Effect-native service and progressively replace Promise internals.

title: Extracted operation and trigger execution service
trigger: manual-cli-migration
finding: Ready-for-dev router, codex playbook execution, planner parsing, and trigger override coercion now live in a dedicated execution module instead of root CLI wiring.
evidence: Added control-plane-execution.ts and removed execution internals from software-factory.ts; root now imports runOperation/fireTrigger services.
followUp: Convert execution service internals from async Promise helpers to direct Effect programs with explicit environment services.

title: Normalize utility script exit contracts to explicit status codes
trigger: manual-tech-debt-cleanup
finding: Utility command execution depended on process.exitCode side effects, creating brittle status propagation through the factory CLI.
evidence: Refactored utility mains to return numeric exit status; removed process.exitCode shim in utility-command-handlers; test:scripts and typecheck pass.
followUp: Continue migrating utility command implementations from argv adapters to effect-first typed command modules.

title: Remove standalone utility script entrypoints from software-factory
trigger: no-backward-compatibility-directive
finding: Utility modules no longer expose argv/main wrappers; only software-factory root CLI remains as executable entry surface.
evidence: Removed process.argv parsing and runScript(main) wrappers from skills/workflows/spec/workflow-memory utility modules; updated script guardrails and tests to enforce single entrypoint.
followUp: Next remove remaining fallback defaults in utility execution paths and migrate IO/process calls to Effect services.

title: Replace utility argv adapters with typed command dispatch
trigger: manual-tech-debt-cleanup
finding: Factory CLI utility commands no longer construct argv arrays; they dispatch typed option objects to utility handlers.
evidence: software-factory/scripts/factory/software-factory.ts and utility-command-handlers.ts now use UtilityCommand union with typed input payloads.
followUp: Migrate utility logic into dedicated Effect command services so script wrappers become thin compatibility shims only.

title: Tagged domain errors enforced in factory control plane
trigger: manual-cli-migration
finding: Factory CLI control-plane paths now throw tagged domain errors and guardrails block regression to throw new Error in factory modules.
evidence: Added CliInputError/RegistryLookupError/RunnerConfigurationError/ExternalToolError/PlannerOutputError/RoutingConstraintError and replaced all throw new Error sites in software-factory.ts; script guardrails now enforce no throw new Error in factory/*.ts.
followUp: Move operation/trigger/doctor logic into dedicated Effect service modules and keep extending tagged errors into utility domains.

title: Typed operation and trigger run inputs
trigger: manual-cli-migration

evidence: Introduced OperationRunOptions/OperationRunInput/TriggerFireInput types, strict trigger arg parsing, and executeOperationRun/executeTriggerFire Effect handlers.
followUp: Extract operation/trigger/doctor business logic from root file into dedicated Effect service modules with tagged domain errors.

title: Backend docs conformity guardrails expanded

reflection: Automated checks on canonical references prevent silent drift better than manual review alone.
feedback: Conformity guardrails are now stronger for backend docs discoverability and lifecycle consistency.

title: Backend docs coverage audit and drift findings
trigger: Request to evaluate backend docs depth across architecture layers and Effect patterns
finding: Core backend patterns are strong (use-case/repo/handler/error/runtime/authz), but key gaps exist in DB design/migration guidance, external service resilience patterns, and explicit transaction/concurrency playbooks; detected drift between observability docs and datadog lifecycle guidance.
evidence: docs/patterns/*.md, docs/architecture/{overview,access-control,observability,datadog}.md, docs/master-spec.md, packages/api/src/server/runtime.ts, packages/db/src/telemetry.ts, apps/{server,worker}/src/{services.ts,worker.ts,server.ts}
followUp: Create prescriptive backend docs for schema/migration/index strategy, provider timeout/retry/idempotency policy, and transaction boundaries; reconcile telemetry lifecycle docs into one canonical source and add docs consistency invariant.
reflection: Reviewing docs with code-side validation caught high-impact drift early and clarified which layers are inventory vs normative guidance.
feedback: Docs quality is high for request flow and Effect layering but backend operations/persistence guidance needs stronger normative standards and guardrail tests.

title: Backend pattern docs hardening and drift guardrails
trigger: User requested implementation of full backend docs improvement plan
finding: Added prescriptive backend patterns for schema design, external service resilience, transaction boundaries, and worker runtime; reconciled telemetry lifecycle wording and ownership guidance drift; added invariant checks for telemetry-doc lifecycle consistency and integration-test inventory sync.
evidence: docs/patterns/{database-schema,external-service-resilience,transaction-boundaries,worker-runtime}.md, docs/architecture/{datadog,observability,overview}.md, docs/patterns/use-case.md, docs/testing/{integration-tests,invariants}.md, packages/testing/src/__tests__/docs-invariants.test.ts

reflection: Converting inventory-level docs into normative patterns plus invariants gives faster, safer agent and human execution.

title: Best Practice Researcher: backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository — podcast prepared statements gap, annotateUseCaseSpan type-safety
trigger: Random-walk best-practice-researcher automation run targeting meso | backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository domain
finding: Podcast repo is the only repo missing prepared statements for findById/findByIdForUser (issue #225 implementation missed it). annotateUseCaseSpan userId is optional with implicit getCurrentUser fallback, creating a latent runtime error risk in worker/system contexts.
evidence: packages/media/src/podcast/repos/podcast-repo.reads.ts (no prepared() import/usage); packages/media/src/shared/safety-primitives.ts:84-110 (UseCaseSpanInput.userId optional with getCurrentUser fallback)
followUp: Issue #276: Add prepared statements to podcast repo. Issue #277: Make annotateUseCaseSpan userId type-safe for worker contexts. Both awaiting human ready-for-dev label.
reflection: Thorough codebase audit confirmed strong overall compliance: 50+ use cases all have span instrumentation, ownership checks, and safety primitive usage. The prepared statement gap in podcast repo is a concrete miss from the #225 rollout.
feedback: External research on Effect v4 beta was informative for future planning but did not yield immediate actionable findings for the current v3 codebase.

title: Best Practice Researcher: coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions — getData() observability gap, persona parallelization, mock factory cleanup
trigger: Random-walk research: micro/coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions (previous: meso/frontend-react-tanstack-router-query-shadcn)
finding: Three micro-level findings: (1) Multiple domain error classes carry structured fields but lack getData() for telemetry — #266; (2) Sequential persona lookups in podcast generation could be parallelized with Effect.all — #267; (3) Dead createMockInfographicRepo in mock-repos.ts and missing createMockPersonaRepo barrel export (low-signal, no issue).
evidence: packages/media/src/errors/*.ts, packages/media/src/activity/errors.ts, packages/ai/src/errors.ts, packages/queue/src/errors.ts, packages/db/src/errors.ts — getData() gap. packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-script.ts:87-111, generate-audio.ts:99-107 — sequential persona lookups. packages/media/src/test-utils/mock-repos.ts:196-227 vs mock-infographic-repo.ts — duplicate factory.
followUp: Track #266 and #267 for implementation after human ready-for-dev approval. Next run should target an uncovered domain: architecture-boundaries, backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository, api-contracts-orpc-hono, or ai-sdk-and-provider-integration.
reflection: Micro scope produced concrete but smaller-impact findings. Codebase discipline is excellent — Effect TS patterns, Layer rules, and safety invariants all 100% compliant.
feedback: Micro scope walk-mix was 0% before this run vs 30% target. Should prioritize micro 1-2 more times to rebalance. External research confirmed existing patterns are aligned with community best practices.

title: Best Practice Researcher: frontend-react-tanstack-router-query-shadcn — SSE query key duplication, MutationCache fallback, persona entity gap, QueryErrorResetBoundary
trigger: Random-walk protocol selected meso/frontend-react-tanstack-router-query-shadcn (not covered in last 8 runs)
finding: 4 actionable findings: (1) SSE handlers duplicate query key helpers from feature hooks, (2) No global MutationCache.onError fallback — at least 3 mutations silently swallow errors, (3) Persona missing from entityQueryKeys in SSE handlers, (4) SuspenseBoundary lacks QueryErrorResetBoundary for proper error recovery
evidence: sse-handlers.ts:21-50 (8 duplicated query key helpers), use-start-research.ts (no onError), use-synthesize-research.ts (no callbacks), use-synthesize-persona.ts (no callbacks), suspense-boundary.tsx (no QueryErrorResetBoundary)
followUp: Issues #255, #256, #257, #258 created. Next run should target micro scope or ai-sdk domain to improve walk coverage.
reflection: Parallel research agents (codebase + external) yielded thorough coverage. External research confirmed SSE/mutation patterns align with TkDodo recommendations. All 4 findings have concrete repo evidence.
feedback: Micro scope remains at 0% coverage. Next 1-2 runs should target micro/coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions.

title: Issue evaluator run: 10 issues evaluated, 3 ready-for-dev, 7 human-eval-needed
trigger: Scheduled issue-evaluator automation run on 2026-03-01
finding: Evaluated 10 unlabeled open issues against strict readiness rubric. Approved 3 user-facing UX improvements at cap (#268 discard confirmation, #270 regeneration confirmation, #269 preset surfacing). Deferred 7 to human-eval-needed: 2 unresolved design choices (#272, #263), 2 cap overflow candidates (#267, #262), 2 developer-tooling items (#266, #271), 1 medium-effort engineering quality initiative (#264). Zero rejected. Approval rate 30% (3/10) — conservative, within cap.
evidence: Issues evaluated: #268, #270, #269 (ready-for-dev); #272, #271, #267, #266, #264, #263, #262 (human-eval-needed). Rubric applied: user pain targeting, scope boundedness, evidence concreteness, testable AC, no new subsystem, no enterprise governance. Pre-existing labeled issues untouched: #261, #260, #259, #258, #257, #256, #255, #251, #250, #237, #233, #211, #210.
followUp: Cap overflow candidates #267 (parallelize persona lookups) and #262 (stop swallowing avatar failures) are strong next-cycle approvals. #272 and #263 need human design decisions before dev. #264 needs scope reduction to first increment. Observability items (#266, #271) suitable for batch.
reflection: Rubric applied consistently; cap constraint forced clear prioritization by user impact. All 3 approved issues share same low-effort/high-UX-impact profile (confirmation dialogs, preset surfacing) that are safe for automation pickup.
feedback: No issues were rejected this run — all had reasonable value propositions. Future runs should watch for accumulation of human-eval-needed backlog and flag if deferred items are not being resolved.

title: Issue evaluator run: 14 issues evaluated, 3 approved, 4 newly labeled
trigger: Scheduled issue-evaluator automation run
finding: Evaluated 14 open issues. Decision mix: 3 ready-for-dev (#259, #260, #258 — a11y and error recovery fixes), 4 newly human-eval-needed (#261, #256, #255, #257 — qualified but capped or indirect benefit), 7 unchanged human-eval-needed (product-vision and medium-high effort items). Zero rejected. Approval rate bounded at 3/14 (21%) per run cap. All 3 approved issues are low-effort, high-signal fixes on existing product surfaces with concrete file-level evidence.
evidence: ready-for-dev: #259 (chat a11y WCAG Level A), #260 (podcast aria-live gap), #258 (SuspenseBoundary error recovery). human-eval-needed new: #261 (list page a11y, capped), #256 (MutationCache onError, capped), #255 (SSE query key dedup, indirect), #257 (persona SSE, preventive). Product-vision issues (#233, #211, #210) remain human-eval-needed — net-new surfaces or cross-surface architecture. #237 (worker Redis) remains human-eval-needed — architecture change.

title: Product Owner Reviewer: accessibility and keyboard/focus/usability — chat input accessible names, podcast action bar aria-live parity, list loading state announcements
trigger: Scheduled product-owner-reviewer automation run — micro scope, accessibility-keyboard-focus-usability domain. Previous 6 runs covered meso/micro across chat-ai-assistant, infographic, terminology, empty-loading-error, onboarding, approval-retry. Accessibility domain was uncovered and adjacent.
finding: Micro-scope a11y review of high-traffic screens found 6 ranked recommendations. Top 3 filed as issues: (1) Chat send buttons and textareas lack accessible names — WCAG Level A violations on the primary AI interaction surface across research-chat-dialog, persona-chat-dialog, and writing-assistant-panel (impact HIGH, effort LOW, confidence 0.95). (2) Podcast action bar missing aria-live parity — voiceover/infographic bars have role=status aria-live=polite from PR #238, but podcast global-action-bar was missed (impact HIGH, effort LOW, confidence 0.90). (3) List page loading/filtering states silent to screen readers — 4 list pages use opacity-70 visual cue but no aria-busy or result count announcements (impact MEDIUM, effort LOW, confidence 0.85). Additional lower-priority findings: document processing state missing role=status, error/suspense fallback roles, persona form aria-required and quote input labels.
evidence: Chat inputs: research-chat-dialog.tsx:251-258 (send button no aria-label), persona-chat-dialog.tsx:226-233 (same), writing-assistant-panel.tsx:120-126 (same), research-chat-dialog.tsx:234-250 (textarea placeholder-only). Podcast bar: global-action-bar.tsx:129-150 (no aria-live) vs voiceover action-bar.tsx:58 (has aria-live). List loading: document-list.tsx:270, voiceover-list.tsx:362, podcast-list.tsx:180, infographic-list.tsx:186 (isPending opacity but no aria-busy). Positive: skip link layout.tsx:321-326, landmarks, audio player ARIA slider, roving tabindex voice-selector.tsx, PR #254 list roles.
followUp: Issues filed: #259 (chat input accessible names), #260 (podcast action bar aria-live), #261 (list loading state announcements). All awaiting human ready-for-dev label. Open issue #251 covers chat streaming output aria-live (complementary). Lower-priority follow-ups: document processing state role=status, error/suspense fallback roles, persona form aria-required. Next trajectory: meso scope, create-edit-generate flow for document or podcast.
reflection: Accessibility domain was a productive unexplored area. The codebase has good foundational a11y (Radix primitives, skip links, landmarks, recent PR #254 improvements) but gaps in dynamic state announcements and input labeling. Exploring all three surface types (list, detail, chat) in parallel via agents gave comprehensive coverage.
feedback: Could have checked the streaming indicator in chat-message.tsx more carefully — the visual-only pulse span is a clear gap that #251 partially addresses but streaming-complete announcements are still missing.

title: Product Owner Reviewer: podcast create/edit/generate flow — discard confirmation, instruction preset parity, regeneration warning
trigger: product-owner-reviewer automation run — meso scope, create-edit-generate-podcast domain
finding: Reviewed full podcast create/edit/generate user journey from setup wizard through workbench editing to generation and approval. Found 4 ranked UX friction points: (1) Discard button in script-panel.tsx clears unsaved edits with no confirmation dialog, despite delete having confirmation — high impact, low effort; (2) 6 instruction presets available in setup wizard step-instructions.tsx are absent from workbench podcast-settings.tsx, forcing users to manually type generation style when iterating — medium impact, low effort; (3) handleSave in use-podcast-actions.ts silently triggers full regeneration (overwriting script+audio) when documents/script-settings change, with only a post-hoc toast — medium impact, low effort; (4) Voice selection uses radio grid in setup vs dropdown in workbench — lower priority, defensible design choice.
evidence: script-panel.tsx discard button fires onDiscard() directly; use-script-editor.ts discardChanges() immediately clears draft state. step-instructions.tsx defines 6 PRESETS (Conversational, Key takeaways, Educational, Add humor, Deep dive, Quick summary); podcast-settings.tsx shows only textarea. use-podcast-actions.ts lines 88-121 branch on documentSelection.hasChanges||settings.hasScriptSettingsChanges for full regeneration with toast only in onSuccess callback. Compared against docs/frontend/components.md container/presenter pattern, docs/frontend/error-handling.md confirmation patterns, and docs/master-spec.md podcast domain surface.
followUp: Created issues #268 (discard confirmation, rank 1), #269 (instruction presets in workbench, rank 2), #270 (regeneration confirmation, rank 3). All awaiting human ready-for-dev label. Voice selection inconsistency (rank 4) noted but not filed — defensible in workbench context. Next adjacent domain: create/edit/generate flow for voiceover or document.
reflection: Thorough exploration of full podcast journey from list to setup wizard to workbench to generation to approval revealed multiple bounded, actionable UX improvements that follow existing patterns (ConfirmationDialog, preset components). All three issues target real user friction in the edit-iterate-regenerate loop.
feedback: Consider exploring voiceover or document create/edit/generate flow next to compare setup/workbench parity patterns across content surfaces.

title: Best Practice Researcher: observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle — instrumentation teardown + handler request.id propagation
trigger: Random-walk weighted adjacent transition from previous meso/ai-sdk-and-provider-integration to meso/observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle (walk mode: weighted-adjacent, no forced docs override).
finding: Two high-signal, non-duplicate findings. (1) TelemetryLive registers ORPC instrumentation but does not retain/unload the registerInstrumentations cleanup handle, so instrumentation lifecycle is not fully scoped to runtime.dispose(). (2) Observability docs require request.id on handler spans, but handleEffectWithProtocol only annotates request.id in the optional manual span branch; routers pass requestId while relying on @orpc/otel auto spans. Coverage snapshot (last 8 before this run): meso/ai-sdk-and-provider-integration, macro/docs-and-guardrail-drift, meso/backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository, micro/coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions, meso/frontend-react-tanstack-router-query-shadcn, macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config, meso/database-drizzle-postgres, macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config (macro=3, meso=4, micro=1). Chosen path this run: meso/observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle. Signal score: 4/5. Followability delta: +2.
evidence: packages/db/src/telemetry.ts:185-197 (registerInstrumentations return ignored; finalizer only forceFlush+shutdown), docs/architecture/observability.md:27-36 (request.id required on handler spans), packages/api/src/server/effect-handler.ts:264-273 (request.id only on optional span branch), packages/api/src/server/router/chat.ts:17-67 (requestId passed without manual span), packages/api/src/server/__tests__/chat-handler.invariants.test.ts:31-36 (asserts requestId plumbing, not span attribute propagation). External research: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-js-contrib/blob/main/packages/opentelemetry-instrumentation/src/autoLoader.ts, https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/protocol/exporter/, https://orpc.unnoq.com/docs/open-telemetry
followUp: Related prior issues reviewed for dedupe/context: #45, #78, #154, #272. New issues opened: #327 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/327 and #328 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/328. No open duplicate issue/PR found for either recommendation.
reflection: Two-pass protocol stayed bounded: lightweight scan identified candidate drift and lifecycle gaps; deep pass expanded only observability runtime + api handler contracts with one adjacent docs domain. Dedupe checks across open/closed issues and open PRs prevented reopening settled OTLP-contract work.
feedback: Observability coverage is now refreshed after recent ai/docs/backend/frontend scans. Next weighted walk can target architecture-boundaries (macro) or api-contracts-orpc-hono (meso) unless #327/#328 require high-priority follow-on validation.

evidence: packages/auth/src/server/auth.ts; packages/auth/src/server/oauth-token-crypto.ts; packages/auth/src/server/microsoft-role-sync.ts; packages/auth/src/__tests__/oauth-token-crypto.test.ts; packages/auth/src/__tests__/microsoft-role-sync.test.ts; packages/auth/src/__tests__/auth.test.ts; packages/api/src/server/effect-handler.ts; packages/api/src/server/__tests__/effect-handler.invariants.test.ts; docs/architecture/security.md; pnpm --filter @repo/auth test; pnpm --filter @repo/api test:invariants; pnpm --filter @repo/auth typecheck; pnpm --filter @repo/api typecheck; pnpm test:invariants

followUp: Parse deep research token usage if Google interactions responses expose usage metadata, then enable deep-research estimatedCostUsdMicros using the existing typed pricing definition
reflection: Pricing needs to live with the model definition, not the UI or admin read model, otherwise drift is guaranteed when models change
feedback: Require pricing metadata in the model catalog and keep provider recorders thin; that gives compile-time pressure to add prices whenever a model ID changes

title: Repo security audit found defense-in-depth gaps
trigger: User requested full security audit of Content Studio codebase
finding: Confirmed OAuth provider tokens are stored in plaintext at rest; SSRF validation is vulnerable to DNS rebinding between validation and fetch; office-file parsing lacks decompression-ratio limits; output-validation logs can dump response bodies; asset proxy tokens reuse the auth secret by default; AI-heavy endpoints lack per-user quotas.
evidence: packages/db/src/schemas/auth.ts; packages/auth/src/server/microsoft-role-sync.ts; packages/media/src/source/services/url-validator.ts; packages/media/src/source/services/url-scraper-impl.ts; packages/media/src/source/parsers.ts; packages/api/src/server/effect-handler.ts; apps/server/src/env.ts; packages/media/src/source/use-cases/create-from-research.ts; packages/api/src/server/router/chat.ts
followUp: Encrypt provider refresh/access tokens, pin resolved IPs or fetch by resolved address for URL ingestion, add archive expansion limits for DOCX/PPTX/PDF parsing, redact response payloads from validation logs, split asset-signing secret from auth secret, and add per-user cost/concurrency quotas for research/chat.

title: Product Owner Reviewer: approval/retry micro pass — infographic approval drift, title persistence on generate, and SSE failure-message safety
trigger: Advisory-lane product-owner-reviewer run from automations/product-owner-reviewer/product-owner-reviewer.md with weighted adjacent transition from meso/chat-ai-assistant-clarity to micro/approval-review-retry.
finding: Micro approval/review/retry pass found three high-signal UX cohesion gaps in active generation workflows. (1) Infographic approvals can remain marked approved across regeneration, unlike podcast/voiceover approval-reset behavior, which weakens trust in review state and can mislead teams about what still needs re-approval. (2) Infographic title drafts are not persisted on Generate/Retry, so generation and completion notifications can continue using stale titles, creating identity drift during review loops. (3) SSE completion failure toasts can surface raw backend/provider error text, creating inconsistent and overly technical retry messaging versus frontend safe-error guidance.
evidence: Docs baseline reviewed: docs/frontend/project-structure.md, docs/frontend/components.md, docs/frontend/error-handling.md, docs/master-spec.md. Route/component evidence: apps/web/src/features/infographics/components/infographic-workbench-container.tsx:365,384-389,464-468,483-498 and apps/web/src/features/infographics/hooks/use-infographic-actions.ts:84-90 show approval/title/generate orchestration behavior; apps/web/src/features/infographics/components/infographic-item.tsx:147-151 confirms list-level approved signal from approvedBy. Backend parity evidence: packages/media/src/infographic/use-cases/generate-infographic.ts:43-46 does not clear approval while packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/start-generation.ts:57 and packages/media/src/voiceover/use-cases/start-generation.ts:72 do. State-communication evidence: apps/web/src/shared/hooks/sse-handlers.ts:135-143 and :246-250 uses fetched entity title and raw error passthrough in failure toast path despite safe-message guidance in docs/frontend/error-handling.md:17,165. GitHub dedupe coverage performed against open/closed issues and open PRs via gh issue list (state all) and gh pr list (open).
followUp: Created issues: #358 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/358, #359 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/359, #360 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/360. title: SSO callback review found Better Auth message/code mismatch

title: AI interaction limit plan was written under docs/plans
trigger: User asked for the full implementation plan to be saved in repository docs for a future agent
finding: A detailed future-facing plan now exists in docs/plans/monthly-ai-interaction-limits.md, covering locked product decisions, quota-key taxonomy, data model, enforcement architecture, admin/API surfaces, test coverage, rollout order, and file targets. docs/plans/README.md now links the new plan so later agents can discover it without replaying this discussion.
evidence: docs/plans/monthly-ai-interaction-limits.md; docs/plans/README.md
followUp: If implementation starts later, treat the plan doc as the working design baseline, then update canonical architecture and testing docs only after the feature becomes real system behavior.
reflection: Writing the plan into the repo reduces future re-triage cost and makes the scope decisions durable beyond chat history.
feedback: For future conditional architecture work, prefer saving the decision package in docs/plans immediately once scope, data model, and rollout shape are stable.

title: Generation limits should spend on admission and key by AI modality
trigger: User clarified quota semantics for the generation-limits plan
finding: Quota should be consumed when work is admitted, not only on successful completion, and the control surface should be fine-grained by AI interaction type. The initial taxonomy should support at least llm, tts, and deep_research limit keys so one accepted user action can reserve the relevant modality budget before queued or direct provider execution begins.
evidence: packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/start-generation.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/save-and-queue-audio.ts; packages/media/src/voiceover/use-cases/start-generation.ts; packages/media/src/infographic/use-cases/generate-infographic.ts; packages/media/src/persona/use-cases/generate-avatar.ts; apps/worker/src/unified-worker.ts; packages/ai/src/usage/types.ts
followUp: Model policy keys separately from product entities so one feature can debit multiple modality buckets when needed, keep admission-time checks atomic with enqueue for queued flows, and expose remaining-budget diagnostics per modality in admin tooling.
reflection: This keeps quota semantics aligned with real capacity reservation and avoids rewarding repeated failed attempts or provider instability with free retries.
feedback: Do not key v1 limits off feature names alone such as podcast or voiceover; use modality-aware keys so LLM, TTS, and deep research can be governed independently.

title: Monthly generation limits should use policy config plus a dedicated attempt ledger
trigger: User requested a plan for global and per-user monthly generation limits across generation features
finding: The existing job queue and ai_usage_event ledger already capture user ownership, request/job scope, and generation activity, but ai_usage_event is provider-facing audit and cost telemetry and can emit multiple rows for one user action. The safer quota design is a media-layer guard that checks effective monthly policy and records one dedicated usage or attempt row per user-triggered generation start, with admin-managed global defaults and per-user overrides.
evidence: packages/db/src/schemas/jobs.ts; packages/db/src/schemas/ai-usage-events.ts; packages/media/src/shared/safety-primitives.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/start-generation.ts; packages/media/src/voiceover/use-cases/start-generation.ts; packages/media/src/infographic/use-cases/generate-infographic.ts; packages/media/src/persona/use-cases/generate-avatar.ts; packages/media/src/admin/use-cases/get-user-detail.ts; apps/web/src/features/admin/components/admin-user-detail-page.tsx
followUp: If this becomes a requirement, implement in phases: define quota taxonomy and monthly-period semantics, add policy and attempt-ledger tables plus admin surfaces, enforce at generation entry use cases before enqueue or provider calls, then add use-case, router, workflow, and spec coverage.
reflection: The repo already has the right seams for ownership, admin-only views, queue-safe start flows, and AI usage observability, so quota enforcement can stay within existing architecture boundaries.
feedback: Do not treat ai_usage_event as the quota source of truth for product limits; count user-intent generation attempts separately so multi-step workflows and retries do not distort entitlements.

title: Auth access-token route must map session lookup failures to 503
trigger: While auditing whether the oRPC interceptor fix had related unexpected-500 cases, the auth token refresh endpoint was reviewed.
finding: The exact Effect.runSync/onError interceptor bug was isolated to the API OpenAPI handler, but the cookie-backed /api/auth/access-token route still used Effect.runPromise directly. If Better Auth session lookup threw AuthSessionLookupError, Hono's global error handler would convert that expected service-unavailable auth infrastructure failure into a generic 500.
evidence: Changed apps/server/src/routes/auth.ts to use Effect.runPromiseExit(getSessionAccessToken(...)), log a stable AUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN_FAILURE tag with the failure tag, and return 503 on Failure while preserving 401 for no-session and 204 for valid token reissue. Added apps/server/src/__tests__/auth-route.test.ts covering 204 valid session, 401 missing session, and 503 lookup failure.
followUp: For non-oRPC Hono routes that call Effect directly, prefer runPromiseExit plus explicit status mapping whenever the Effect error channel represents expected transport outcomes.

title: oRPC auth middleware must preserve 401s through OpenAPI client interceptors
trigger: User reported missing-session requests returning HTTP 500 instead of 4xx after reload-related auth work.
finding: The protectedProcedure auth branch was expected to throw UNAUTHORIZED, but createApi wrapped client interceptor error handling in Effect.runSync(handleORPCError(...)). That turned passthrough oRPC errors into FiberFailure wrappers, so OpenAPI encoded them as 500s and the web app never hit its normal unauthorized logout path.
evidence: Restored protectedProcedure to throw contract-native errors.UNAUTHORIZED, changed createApi clientInterceptors to call handleORPCError without Effect.runSync, made handleORPCError log-only so it does not replace the original protocol error, and added an HTTP-level createApi regression test proving GET /api/events returns 401 for missing sessions.
followUp: Keep server-side onError interceptors non-throwing unless they can preserve the original transport error object; otherwise expected auth failures will be upgraded to 500s.

title: Persona AI draft uses save-oriented CTA and redirect-safe blocker
trigger: User reported that the AI-generated persona draft still showed create CTA copy and surfaced the unsaved-changes dialog when saving.
finding: The new persona route treated an AI-generated draft as a create step while the create mutation navigated away with the dirty-form blocker still active.
evidence: Updated PersonaCreate CTA copy to Save Persona/New Persona, moved redirect orchestration into PersonaCreateContainer so navigation happens only after blocker state is disabled, and added regression tests for CTA copy plus redirect blocker behavior.
followUp: Keep create-route navigation inside containers when dirty-state blockers are present so redirects can be coordinated after local state changes.

title: Podcast episode plan output now normalizes invalid estimatedMinutes before API serialization
trigger: User reported generate-plan and patch requests returning 500 because episodePlan sections contained estimatedMinutes: 0, which violated the response contract.
finding: The write-side sanitizer already intended to drop non-positive estimatedMinutes, but API serialization still trusted the stored entity shape. Adding episode-plan normalization inside the podcast serializer prevents invalid stored plans from tripping contract validation, and the generate-plan regression test now asserts that estimatedMinutes: 0 is removed before persistence.
evidence: packages/db/src/schemas/podcasts.ts; packages/db/src/__tests__/schema-serializers.test.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/__tests__/generate-plan.test.ts
followUp: If invalid episodePlan values are already stored broadly in production, consider a one-off backfill to rewrite persisted JSONB plans into the normalized shape instead of relying on read-time cleanup.

title: Podcast episodePlan now ships as a forward migration and schema docs forbid editing baseline migrations
trigger: User asked to correct the migration history for the new podcast column and update docs so future schema changes require proper migrations.
finding: The episodePlan column had been added by editing the 0000 baseline SQL and snapshot instead of creating a forward migration. The fix restored 0000 as the bootstrap baseline, generated 0001_add-podcast-episode-plan.sql plus matching snapshot/journal updates, and updated the database-schema and enum-constants docs to require new numbered migrations for post-bootstrap schema changes and treat db:push as local apply only.
evidence: packages/db/drizzle/0001_add-podcast-episode-plan.sql; packages/db/drizzle/meta/0001_snapshot.json; packages/db/drizzle/meta/_journal.json; docs/patterns/database-schema.md; docs/patterns/enum-constants.md
followUp: If older repos have similar drift, audit for schema changes represented only by edits to 0000 or prior snapshots and regenerate forward migrations before release.

title: Podcast script prompt now carries target runtime and planner section count is duration-aware
trigger: User asked to validate that personas and target length still reach final script generation and that planner section counts are not hard-capped.
finding: Validated personas still flow into final script generation, found targetDurationMinutes was missing from the script prompt, then fixed the prompt wiring. Also removed the planner prompt's hard 3-5 section rule and replaced it with duration-aware guidance that scales section count and total estimated minutes to the target runtime.
evidence: Updated packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-script.ts and packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/podcast-script-system.ts to include targetDurationMinutes in final script generation; updated packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/podcast-plan-system.ts to remove the fixed section cap; added use-case tests asserting host/co-host personas plus target runtime reach generationPrompt and planner system prompt no longer contains 'Produce 3 to 5 sections'.
followUp: If product wants tighter pacing control later, add a post-plan invariant that compares summed section estimatedMinutes against targetDurationMinutes with a tolerance instead of relying only on prompt guidance.

title: Podcast SSE now exposes script-ready phase and durable failure state
trigger: User reported podcast script and audio appearing simultaneously in the UI
finding: Full generate-podcast ran script then audio inside one worker job, but SSE only emitted terminal completion events and generic job failures did not persist failed entity state, so the UI skipped the script-ready phase and could refetch stale generating_* statuses.
evidence: apps/worker/src/handlers/handlers.ts; apps/worker/src/unified-worker.ts; apps/worker/src/entity-failure.ts; apps/web/src/shared/hooks/sse-handlers.ts; packages/api/src/server/router/__tests__/events.integration.test.ts
followUp: Keep emitting intermediate entity_change events for multi-phase jobs and add replay/dedup tests whenever SSE resume semantics change.
reflection: The backend phase transitions were already correct; the missing piece was surfacing them as explicit SSE contract boundaries.
feedback: Treat script-ready and failure-state SSE updates as first-class contract points for generation workflows.

title: Podcast wizard now splits quick-start setup directions from optional episode planning
trigger: User wanted the generic final instructions step replaced with a fast path plus an optional AI-planner step without losing source grounding.
finding: Implemented a 4-step podcast setup flow: Sources, Audio, Quick Start, Episode Planner. Added persisted setupInstructions for setup-time directions, kept promptInstructions for later workbench regenerate directions, made planner auto-generate on entry, and added quick-start generation that can ignore a saved plan while still grounding every plan and script run in the source materials.
evidence: Changed apps/web podcast setup wizard and added step-quick-start UI; added podcast.setupInstructions schema + 0002_add-podcast-setup-instructions migration; generate-plan now includes setupInstructions in the planning prompt; generate/start-generation/worker queue now support quick-start overrides with ignoreEpisodePlan; added db/media/api/web regression coverage.
followUp: If users revisit Quick Start after generating but not approving a plan, monitor whether the explicit skip-plan behavior is intuitive enough or if the product needs an explicit draft-plan/approved-plan distinction later.

title: Podcast wizard now supports optional AI plan approval before generation
trigger: User approved replacing the final podcast wizard instructions textarea with an AI-assisted planner and requested a skip path for users who do not want to wait.
finding: Implemented a persisted episode-plan flow across db, media, api, and web. The final setup step now lets users generate an AI outline from ready sources, review and edit the angle/hook/closing plus sections, regenerate the plan, or skip planning entirely and generate the podcast immediately. Script generation now consumes the approved plan when present instead of relying only on generic prompt instructions.
evidence: apps/web/src/features/podcasts/components/setup/setup-wizard.tsx; apps/web/src/features/podcasts/components/setup/steps/step-plan.tsx; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-plan.ts; packages/db/src/schemas/podcasts.ts; packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/podcast-plan-system.ts; packages/api/src/contracts/podcasts.ts
followUp: If users need tighter control later, add optional section-level source pin editing and explicit workbench labeling for post-draft regenerate directions so planning and regeneration remain clearly separate.

title: Podcast wizard planning should replace generic instructions with a persisted outline approval step
trigger: Product triage for podcast setup wizard final step
finding: The final wizard step is currently an optional instructions textarea, while the same persisted promptInstructions field is also reused later to guide script regeneration. A better product model is an AI-generated planning step that synthesizes selected sources into an editable outline users approve before script generation, while keeping lightweight regenerate instructions as a separate later control.
evidence: apps/web/src/features/podcasts/components/setup/steps/step-instructions.tsx:34-71; apps/web/src/features/podcasts/components/setup/setup-wizard.tsx:75-188; apps/web/src/features/podcasts/components/workbench/podcast-settings.tsx:365-396; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-script.ts:131-139; packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/podcast-script-system.ts:86-136
followUp: If implemented, add a dedicated planning artifact and preflight generate-outline step instead of overloading promptInstructions, preserve manual editing/approval in the wizard, and keep workbench regeneration directions lightweight and separate.
reflection: The repo already has the right seams for a preflight stage because setup, generation, and later regeneration are distinct surfaces, but the current single-string prompt field blurs those jobs.
feedback: Avoid shipping this as a temporary chat box. Planner state should be persisted and explicitly approved so users understand what the script is about to do.

title: Reload-safe auth now rehydrates bearer state from the Better Auth session
trigger: User reported that Cmd+R / hard refresh logged them out of the web app.
finding: The SPA kept bearer auth in memory only, auth session reads omitted credentials, and the API client only attempted 401 recovery when a bearer token was already present. After a reload, protected loaders had no token and never tried to recover.
evidence: Added credentialed auth-route CORS plus /api/auth/access-token bearer reissue, switched auth client session reads to credentials include, updated refreshAccessToken to rehydrate from the new endpoint, and changed API 401 recovery to retry even when reload cleared in-memory auth. Added regression tests in packages/auth, packages/api, apps/server, and apps/web, and updated auth architecture docs.
followUp: Keep /api/* bearer-only, but treat /api/auth/* as the only place allowed to use the Better Auth session cookie for bearer rehydration so reload continuity does not leak into normal API transport.

title: Restore infographic default content type via presets
trigger: User requested replacing the removed hardcoded infographic prompt framing with an explicit preset menu option and default selection.
finding: Added a dedicated content-type preset group, restored infographic as the implicit default content type, and kept generation behavior aligned by injecting the default infographic style only when no explicit content type is provided.
evidence: Updated apps/web/src/features/infographics/lib/static-presets.ts, apps/web/src/features/infographics/components/preset-picker.tsx, and packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/infographic-generation-user.ts; added coverage in apps/web/src/features/infographics/__tests__/preset-picker.test.tsx and packages/media/src/infographic/__tests__/prompts.test.ts.
followUp: If users start mixing multiple mutually exclusive preset categories, consider promoting category-level exclusive selection beyond content type.

title: Testcontainer schema push should infer drizzle database type from pushSchema
trigger: Monorepo build failed in @repo/testing with a drizzle PgDatabase type mismatch while calling drizzle-kit pushSchema from packages/testing/src/testcontainers/postgres.ts.
finding: The failure was caused by casting the drizzle instance to PgDatabase<any> imported from this package's drizzle-orm dependency, while drizzle-kit/api referenced a different installed drizzle-orm type identity. Replacing the explicit PgDatabase import with Parameters<typeof pushSchema>[1] aligned the cast with the exact type expected by drizzle-kit/api and removed the duplicate-package mismatch.
evidence: packages/testing/src/testcontainers/postgres.ts; node_modules/.pnpm/drizzle-kit@0.31.7/node_modules/drizzle-kit/api.d.ts
followUp: When bridging third-party helper APIs that embed types from transitive dependencies, prefer inferring parameter types from the called API instead of importing the same-looking type from a sibling package path.

title: Voiceover assistant now forces rewrite tool on direct edit requests
trigger: User requested the voiceover writing assistant to be more proactive about tool-calling while editing scripts
finding: The writing assistant already exposed updateVoiceoverText, but the runtime left tool selection fully automatic. Direct rewrite requests could stay conversational instead of applying edits, so the fix combined stronger prompt instructions with a conditional toolChoice that requires updateVoiceoverText only on fresh user turns that clearly ask for script changes.
evidence: packages/ai/src/llm/service.ts; packages/ai/src/llm/providers/google.ts; packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/stream-writing-assistant-chat.ts; packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/__tests__/stream-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts; packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/chat-writing-assistant-system.ts
followUp: If users need localized suggestions instead of full-transcript rewrites, add a selection-scoped edit tool rather than weakening the direct-edit forcing rule.
reflection: Prompt guidance alone was not enough; adding an explicit runtime hint made the assistant behavior more reliable without forcing tools on critique-style turns.
feedback: When a chat feature has one safe mutating tool and a clear user intent signal, prefer conditional toolChoice over hoping the model infers the right action from prompt text alone.

title: Voiceover unsaved state now uses a single Save & Regenerate action
trigger: User requested removing the unnecessary Save Draft button from the voiceover workbench
finding: The voiceover action bar exposed both Save Draft and Save & Regenerate even though generation already persists pending edits. The fix removed the standalone Save Draft action and the hidden Cmd/Ctrl+S shortcut so unsaved voiceover changes now funnel through the single visible Save & Regenerate path.
evidence: apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/components/workbench/action-bar.tsx; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/components/voiceover-detail.tsx; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/components/voiceover-detail-container.tsx; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/action-bar.test.tsx; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/voiceover-detail-container.test.tsx
followUp: If title-only draft persistence becomes necessary later, add an explicit new action with a clear label instead of reintroducing a second generic save control.
reflection: Matching the simpler podcast workbench pattern removed UI clutter and avoided a hidden shortcut path.
feedback: When a workbench already saves inside its primary generate action, avoid exposing a second standalone save control unless it serves a distinct user outcome.

title: Voiceover workbench needs bottom clearance and explicit rewrite confirmation
trigger: User reported that the save bar obscured long script content and the writing assistant gave no clear confirmation after applying edits
finding: The voiceover detail content ended too close to the persistent action bar during long-form editing, and tool-applied transcript rewrites relied on the model's follow-up turn for confirmation. The fix adds extra bottom clearance in the voiceover content wrapper and appends an immediate assistant confirmation message after a successful transcript tool application.
evidence: apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/components/voiceover-detail.tsx; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/hooks/use-writing-assistant-chat.ts; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/voiceover-detail.test.tsx; apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/use-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts
followUp: If the workbench gets more persistent bottom surfaces, centralize the bottom inset as a shared workbench token rather than repeating per-feature padding adjustments.
reflection: Keeping the clearance change in the voiceover presenter avoided broad layout churn, while the hook-level confirmation made the tool application state explicit without needing backend changes.
feedback: When AI tools mutate editor state asynchronously, the UI should emit its own success confirmation instead of depending solely on a second model turn to explain what happened.

title: Worker global concurrency default raised from 5 to 20
trigger: User requested increasing the worker job limit from 5 to 20
finding: The worker's only hard global job cap lives in apps/worker/src/constants.ts as MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS. Raising that default to 20 increases concurrent job processing capacity without changing per-type concurrency rules or queue ownership semantics.
evidence: apps/worker/src/constants.ts; docs/patterns/job-queue.md; apps/worker/src/base-worker.ts; apps/worker/src/unified-worker.ts
followUp: If throughput still feels capped in production, revisit per-type concurrency overrides and worker DB pool sizing based on observed saturation rather than changing them preemptively.
reflection: This change was isolated to the shared worker default and its canonical queue doc, which kept the write set small despite a heavily modified worktree.
feedback: For simple capacity changes, confirm whether the request targets the global worker cap or a product-level admission limit before widening scope.

title: Best Practice Researcher: api-contracts-orpc-hono — BAD_GATEWAY semantic collapse vs SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE fallback
trigger: Advisory-lane best-practice-researcher run from automations/best-practice-researcher/best-practice-researcher.md with weighted-adjacent meso->meso transition from backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository to api-contracts-orpc-hono.
finding: Walk mode: weighted-adjacent transition (meso->meso). Previous path: scope=meso,domain=backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository. Chosen path: scope=meso,domain=api-contracts-orpc-hono. Revisit justification: not a same-path reuse. Coverage snapshot (last 8 BPR runs before this run): meso/ai-sdk-and-provider-integration; meso/observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle; macro/architecture-boundaries; macro/docs-and-guardrail-drift; macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config; meso/api-contracts-orpc-hono; macro/docs-and-guardrail-drift; meso/backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository (macro=4, meso=4, micro=0). High-signal non-duplicate finding: BAD_GATEWAY semantics are defined in domain errors but collapsed at API boundary because appContract omits BAD_GATEWAY and effect fallback prefers SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE for 502. Opened #383. Existing high-signal issues #367 and #368 remain open; #367 was extended with fresh reproducibility evidence instead of creating a duplicate. Signal score: 4/5. Followability delta: +2.
evidence: Repo evidence: packages/db/src/errors.ts:128-143 (ExternalServiceError uses httpStatus 502 + httpCode BAD_GATEWAY); packages/api/src/contracts/index.ts:69-73 (SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE mapped to status 502, BAD_GATEWAY missing); packages/api/src/server/effect-handler.ts:73-85 (502 fallback order SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE then BAD_GATEWAY); packages/api/src/server/__tests__/effect-handler.invariants.test.ts:64-71 (invariant codifies 502->SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE preference); docs/patterns/error-handling.md:95 (503 service unavailable guidance) and docs/testing/invariants.md:58 (502->SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE docs). Local repro command output: {"code":"SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE","opts":{"message":"upstream failed"}} when a BAD_GATEWAY-like 502 error is mapped without BAD_GATEWAY factory availability. External research links: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110#section-15.6.3 ; https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110#section-15.6.4 ; https://orpc.dev/docs/openapi/error-handling
followUp: New issue: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/383. Related open issues: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/367 (commented with fresh repro: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/367#issuecomment-4022491940) and https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/368. Open PR dedupe check: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/pull/129 has no overlap. Wait for human triage; do not add ready-for-dev from this lane. Next walk should rebalance toward micro/coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions or adjacent meso/database-drizzle-postgres unless api follow-up urgency stays >=4.

Dedupe checks via gh: issue search over open/closed for failStaleJobs/job_processing_idx/stale reaper found no exact duplicate; open PR check found only #129 with no overlap. title: Product Owner Reviewer: approval/retry micro pass — phase-aware Deep Research retries and deterministic synthesis handoff
trigger: Advisory-lane product-owner-reviewer run from automations/product-owner-reviewer/product-owner-reviewer.md with weighted transition from meso/create-edit-generate-voiceover to micro/approval-review-retry
finding: Top recommendations: (1) keep #212 prioritized so research/persona synthesis output is previewed before commit; (2) make Deep Research retry phase-aware and deterministic by reusing successful synthesis payload instead of silently re-synthesizing on every retry (new issue #382); (3) keep #350 prioritized for durable post-start handoff after Deep Research start. User-friction impact: users currently see opaque retry states and can lose confidence that retry preserves intent in the core AI research journey.
evidence: Docs baseline reviewed: docs/frontend/project-structure.md, docs/frontend/components.md, docs/frontend/error-handling.md, docs/master-spec.md. Current-state route/component evidence: research flow merges synthesize+start pending/error in apps/web/src/features/sources/components/research-chat-container.tsx:21-24 and always re-synthesizes before start in :40-48; dialog copy is phase-blind in apps/web/src/features/sources/components/research-chat-dialog.tsx:182-183 and :193; shared confirmation messaging is generic in apps/web/src/shared/components/chat-auto-trigger-confirmation.tsx:24-30; persona synthesis still commits without structured preview in apps/web/src/features/personas/components/persona-chat-container.tsx:55-63; sources post-start handoff remains toast-only in apps/web/src/features/sources/hooks/use-start-research.ts:11-16 and immediate close in research-chat-container.tsx:45. GitHub dedupe checks executed via gh issue list (state all, targeted searches) and gh pr list (open).
followUp: Opened issue: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/382 (awaits human ready-for-dev). Reused existing open issues with fresh evidence comments: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/212#issuecomment-4020962998 and https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/350#issuecomment-4020963031. Next trajectory suggestion: micro terminology consistency for chat confirmation/retry copy once #212/#382 direction is approved.

title: Worker concurrency should be budgeted, not fixed at 5
trigger: Reviewed worker max jobs policy for throughput tuning
finding: The worker hardcodes a global concurrency cap of 5 while also using a DB pool of 5 and nested per-job fan-out, which makes higher throughput possible only if concurrency, DB pool, and per-type/provider budgets are tuned together.
evidence: apps/worker/src/constants.ts:15; apps/worker/src/worker.ts:50-55; apps/worker/src/base-worker.ts:211-215,332-345; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-script.ts:115-128; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-audio.ts:110-116
followUp: Make worker concurrency and DB pool configurable, reserve slots for heavy job classes, and tune against queue latency, 429s, DB waits, CPU, and memory before raising defaults.

title: Audio playback proxy is now always on
trigger: User requested removing AUDIO_PLAYBACK_PROXY_ENABLED and always proxying audio through the app
finding: The audio playback path no longer carries a feature flag. Server config, env validation, and route rewriting now assume app-proxied audio playback is always enabled, leaving only storage proxying as an optional toggle.
evidence: apps/server/src/audio-playback-proxy.ts; apps/server/src/routes/api.ts; apps/server/src/services.ts; apps/server/src/env.ts; apps/server/src/__tests__/audio-playback-proxy.test.ts; apps/server/src/__tests__/env.test.ts; docs/architecture/security.md; docs/architecture/deployment-env-matrix.md; docs/architecture/eks-ingress-env-template.md; apps/server/.env.example
followUp: If media proxy behavior changes again, keep env docs and runtime validation aligned so deployment templates do not drift from enforced server behavior.

title: Compose credentials moved out of tracked YAML
trigger: Git commit secret scanning flagged hardcoded Docker Compose DB credentials
finding: compose.yaml embedded tracked DB and MinIO credential literals, so secret scanning tripped even on default local values. The fix moves those values to env-backed compose inputs and keeps local setup/e2e flows injecting the same defaults at runtime.
evidence: compose.yaml; deploy; scripts/compose-runtime-env.ts; scripts/setup-local.ts; scripts/testing/run-e2e-tests.ts; LINUX-DEPLOY.md; software-factory/scripts/setup-env.sh
followUp: If secret scanning expands to example env files, move local example credential defaults behind the same helper/env-file pattern to keep tracked defaults out of commit-scanned files.
reflection: Using env-backed compose interpolation preserved current local behavior while removing the tracked secret-like literals that were tripping the hook.
feedback: When Compose needs local default credentials, keep them in ignored env files or runtime helpers instead of checked-in YAML.

title: Compose deploy credentials use unprefixed env names
trigger: User reported deploy and docker compose requiring CONTENT_STUDIO_-prefixed variables
finding: compose.yaml and the deploy helper were interpolating CONTENT_STUDIO_POSTGRES_* and CONTENT_STUDIO_S3_* for internal Compose PostgreSQL and MinIO credentials. The fix switches the active contract to POSTGRES_USER / POSTGRES_PASSWORD / POSTGRES_DB and S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID / S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, with legacy-prefixed reads kept only as temporary compatibility fallbacks when regenerating env or bootstrapping compose runtime env.
evidence: compose.yaml; deploy; scripts/compose-runtime-env.ts; software-factory/scripts/compose-runtime-env.test.ts; LINUX-DEPLOY.md; software-factory/scripts/setup-env.sh
followUp: Once deploy environments have been refreshed to the new names, remove the temporary legacy fallback reads from deploy and scripts/compose-runtime-env.ts.
reflection: Fixing both compose interpolation and .env.deploy generation in the same patch avoided a split contract where the docs and helper scripts disagreed about the env names.
feedback: When Compose credentials intentionally reuse container env names, keep the deploy script, env bootstrap helper, and operator docs under the same regression test surface.

title: Deep research duplicate worker recovery can regress ready sources
trigger: Debugging local research source doc_9tjja5vgxmznbxdy that completed once then regressed to failed/processing
finding: Worker orphan recovery could enqueue duplicate process-research jobs for the same source, and late stale-job failure sync could overwrite a source that had already reached ready.
evidence: Local Postgres showed doc_9tjja5vgxmznbxdy with wordCount=4706 but researchStatus=in_progress plus jobs job_npkmxyze94gdf10f completed, job_9zf4b0knpe68zdq0/job_b39q39bge47p2c2p failed, and later duplicate processing jobs. Fixed in apps/worker/src/research-recovery.ts and apps/worker/src/entity-failure.ts with regression tests.
followUp: If a source is already stuck from earlier duplicate jobs, restart the worker so the new guards are active and then let one active job settle or clean up the duplicate jobs manually before retrying.

title: Deep research SSE completion path covered with worker and client tests
trigger: Follow-up verification that research completion emits SSE updates after recovering stale process-research jobs
finding: The source SSE completion path existed in code but lacked direct regression coverage for completed process-research jobs. Added a worker publish test and a useSSE routing test.
evidence: apps/worker/src/unified-worker.ts publishes source_job_completion for process-research completions; apps/worker/src/__tests__/unified-worker.test.ts asserts source_job_completion + entity_change; apps/web/src/shared/hooks/__tests__/use-sse.test.ts asserts source_job_completion routes to handleSourceJobCompletion.
followUp: Keep source completion coverage whenever SSE event shapes or worker completion routing changes.

title: Deploy registry now reaches Dockerfile base images
trigger: Deploy flow accepted DOCKER_REGISTRY, but app Dockerfiles still pulled node base images directly from Docker Hub
finding: compose.yaml applied DOCKER_REGISTRY only to pulled service images. The web, server, worker, and db Dockerfiles still used unqualified node base images, so registry selection was incomplete.
evidence: deploy; compose.yaml; apps/server/Dockerfile; apps/web/Dockerfile; apps/worker/Dockerfile; packages/db/Dockerfile; LINUX-DEPLOY.md
followUp: Keep new deploy-time Dockerfiles accepting a shared registry build arg whenever a script or compose layer already exposes registry selection.
reflection: Passing the registry through compose build args kept the existing deploy UX while fixing all image pull paths with a small patch.
feedback: When a deploy script exposes infra configuration like a registry, audit both compose image refs and Dockerfile FROM lines so behavior stays consistent.

title: Deploy script stages corporate proxy CA for builds and runtime
trigger: User asked for the Docker deploy path to work behind a corporate proxy with a custom certificate
finding: Runtime proxy envs already existed in server and worker, but the deploy flow did not stage a CA into containers, did not pass proxy settings into Docker builds, and did not normalize NO_PROXY for compose-internal hosts.
evidence: deploy; compose.yaml; apps/server/Dockerfile; apps/web/Dockerfile; apps/worker/Dockerfile; packages/db/Dockerfile; docker/certs/.gitignore; LINUX-DEPLOY.md; docs/architecture/deployment-env-matrix.md; README.md
followUp: If operators also depend on proxied Docker daemon pulls, document or automate daemon-level proxy and registry-mirror setup separately from container runtime envs.
reflection: Using a gitignored docker/certs asset path let the deploy script feed the same CA file to Docker builds and backend runtime containers without committing certificate material.
feedback: For corporate-proxy support, treat Docker daemon pulls, image builds, and app runtime as separate trust paths and wire all three explicitly.

title: Episode planner runtime now adapts to source volume
trigger: User reported that small and large source documents were producing podcasts with nearly identical runtimes in the episode planner flow.
finding: The planner and script prompts already honor target duration, but the product defaulted drafts to five minutes and gave no content-aware runtime guidance. Added a shared backend recommendation heuristic plus setup-wizard runtime guidance so drafts size themselves to source volume before scripting.
evidence: Added recommendPodcastTargetDurationMinutes in packages/db for backend defaults, removed fixed five-minute auto-podcast creation in process-research, surfaced runtime recommendation in the setup wizard audio step, and passed targeted tests plus pnpm typecheck on 2026-03-10.
followUp: Consider reusing the same recommendation card in post-setup podcast settings so existing drafts expose the same source-volume guidance outside the wizard.

title: Podcast audio generation now sanitizes leading blank segments and hardens multi-speaker TTS prompts
trigger: User reported that the first line of a podcast was sometimes missing in generated audio.
finding: Podcast script segments were passed through to synthesis without trimming, blank-line removal, ordering, or reindexing, so an empty or misordered leading segment could distort the first spoken turn. The Google multi-speaker TTS request also sent only raw speaker-prefixed lines instead of an explicit read-in-order conversation prompt.
evidence: packages/media/src/podcast/script-segments.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-script.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/save-changes.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/generate-audio.ts; packages/ai/src/tts/providers/google.ts; packages/media/src/podcast/use-cases/__tests__/generate-audio.test.ts; packages/ai/src/tts/providers/google.test.ts
followUp: Keep the generate-audio and Google TTS provider regression tests updated if podcast segment persistence or Gemini multi-speaker request formatting changes again.

evidence: Repo evidence: apps/server/src/server.ts:17-23 and apps/worker/src/worker.ts:26-32 fatalExit calls process.exit(1); uncaughtException handlers route into fatalExit (server.ts:35-37, worker.ts:44-46). Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM path disposes runtime (server.ts:111-113, worker.ts:128-130). Telemetry layer finalizer performs forceFlush+shutdown only during runtime disposal in packages/db/src/telemetry.ts:196-203, with lifecycle test at packages/db/src/__tests__/telemetry.test.ts:84-116. Regression trace: issue #153 was closed as completed by PR #166, then commit 864b60bfd65420549a0838e37b1939e221af1ca6 (#236) removed fatal-path cleanup from server/worker. External references: https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-js/modules/_opentelemetry_sdk-node.html (shutdown guidance), https://nodejs.org/api/process.html#warning-using-uncaughtexception-correctly (fatal exception lifecycle guidance).
followUp: New issue opened: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/406. Related prior issue/PR: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/153 and https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/pull/166. title: Align long-running job timeout budgets with stale-reaper and CAS terminal updates
trigger: Issue #377 timeout-budget race between deep research, podcast source waiting, and stale job cleanup
finding: Centralized the long-running job timeout model in @repo/queue, switched source polling loops to wall-clock elapsed time, and guarded terminal queue transitions so late completion cannot overwrite stale failure.
evidence: packages/queue/src/job-timeouts.ts; packages/queue/src/repository.ts; packages/media/src/source/use-cases/await-sources-ready.ts; packages/media/src/source/use-cases/process-research.ts; packages/queue/src/__tests__/claim-job.integration.test.ts
followUp: Watch production logs for late terminal-update warnings and add a dedicated worker-level regression test if duplicate completion events ever recur.
reflection: A single shared timeout model simplified the fix and let queue, worker, docs, and tests converge on one set of constants.
feedback: Avoid reintroducing per-file timeout constants; keep wall-clock budgeting and terminal compare-and-set semantics tied to the shared queue timeout module.

title: Centralized long-running job timeout budgets and guarded terminal queue updates
trigger: Issue #377 implementation for source research and podcast source-wait timeout drift
finding: Source polling budgets had drifted across worker and media code, and terminal queue writes could overwrite a stale-failed job after a late handler completion.
evidence: packages/queue/src/job-timeouts.ts; packages/media/src/source/use-cases/await-sources-ready.ts; packages/media/src/source/use-cases/process-research.ts; packages/queue/src/repository.ts; packages/queue/src/__tests__/claim-job.integration.test.ts
followUp: Reuse the shared @repo/queue timeout constants for future long-running jobs and keep terminal status writes guarded from processing-only states.
reflection: A single timeout model simplified the code and made it straightforward to add high-signal boundary and race tests.
feedback: When a worker and a use case share runtime assumptions, centralize the constants before adding more branch-level fixes.

title: Deploy proxy CA handling now uses NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS only
trigger: User requested removing the duplicate CA_CERT_PATH deploy variable and keeping NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS as the only proxy CA setting.
finding: The deploy flow had split semantics between CA_CERT_PATH for host-side staging and NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS for runtime, which made proxy prompts and saved env state harder to reason about. The deploy script now uses NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS as the single input/output variable and resolves the staged repo cert when existing env files already contain the container path.
evidence: deploy; LINUX-DEPLOY.md; docs/architecture/deployment-env-matrix.md; pnpm test:scripts; bash -n deploy
followUp: Keep deploy prompts and generated env docs aligned whenever proxy or certificate handling changes so the interactive flow does not drift from the runtime env contract.
reflection: Removing the extra variable simplified the proxy surface without changing the staging behavior that Docker builds and backend containers depend on.

title: Podcast generation now uses Gemini 3.1 Pro preview and calibrated script word budgets
trigger: Long-form podcast scripts were consistently under target duration in local postgres evidence for both planned and no-plan runs.
finding: Podcast planning was near target, but script generation under-expanded. Real synthesized conversation episodes were landing around 170-185 spoken words per minute, while the prompt budget assumed 130-160 WPM and the script call used the repo default flash-lite model.
evidence: Checked podcast records in postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres; 5-9 minute targets were often 1.5-5.5 minutes short even when episode plans summed close to target. AI usage logs showed script generations already returned ~1.2k-1.6k output tokens, so the miss was prompt/model quality rather than a trivial token cap.
followUp: Keep podcast generation on a static per-domain model constant, pass per-call model overrides through the AI layer, and calibrate future podcast prompt changes against observed TTS pacing instead of generic spoken-word heuristics.
reflection: A narrow per-call model override was sufficient; there was no need to raise the repo-wide default model or add a retry loop in the first pass.

title: Standardized server boundary problem responses with request correlation
trigger: User request to tackle GitHub issues #367 and #368
finding: Added a shared non-oRPC problem response helper in apps/server so global 500 responses and api body-limit 413 responses now emit machine-readable code plus requestId, while HTTPException responses preserve framework status/body/headers and regain X-Request-Id when middleware set it.
evidence: apps/server/src/problem-response.ts; apps/server/src/error-handler.ts; apps/server/src/routes/api-body-limit.ts; apps/server/src/__tests__/error-handler.test.ts; apps/server/src/__tests__/api-body-limit.test.ts
followUp: If the non-oRPC error standard is expanded beyond these issues, migrate apps/server/src/middleware/rate-limit.ts to the same helper instead of keeping a second JSON envelope.
reflection: One helper kept the envelope and request-id behavior aligned across boundary handlers without touching oRPC contract behavior.
feedback: When boundary handlers bypass Hono context responses, add regression tests that assert both explicit framework headers and correlation headers.

title: Best Practice Researcher: api-contracts-orpc-hono — live SSE starvation risk after replay counter reset across reconnect
trigger: Manual request to execute one full best-practice-researcher run via automations/best-practice-researcher/best-practice-researcher.md advisory profile.
finding: Walk mode: weighted-long-jump transition (meso->meso) with coverage rebalance. Previous path: scope=meso,domain=frontend-react-tanstack-router-query-shadcn. Chosen path: scope=meso,domain=api-contracts-orpc-hono. Revisit justification: not a same-path reuse. Coverage snapshot (last 8 BPR runs before this run): macro/docs-and-guardrail-drift; meso/frontend-react-tanstack-router-query-shadcn; meso/backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository; micro/coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions; macro/docs-and-guardrail-drift; meso/api-contracts-orpc-hono; meso/api-contracts-orpc-hono; macro/docs-and-guardrail-drift (macro=3, meso=4, micro=1). Two-pass outcome: lightweight scan flagged scoped Effect.fork and SSE replay/dedupe edge cases; deep pass validated worker fork is scope-bound but confirmed a new high-signal SSE starvation risk when reconnect cursor is high and replay counter restarts low. Opened non-duplicate issue #428. Signal score: 4/5. Followability delta: +2.
evidence: Repo evidence: packages/api/src/server/replay-buffer.ts uses process-local numeric counter reset (counter=0; push=>String(++counter)); packages/api/src/server/router/events.ts seeds dedupe from lastEventId and skips live events when Number(sseId) <= Number(lastSeenId); apps/web/src/shared/hooks/use-sse.ts persists lastEventId across reconnect. Failure mode: after restart/server-hop with lower local counter, replay may be empty while live ids start low and are suppressed as stale, dropping real-time updates until counter surpasses stale client cursor. Existing tests cover connected-sentinel regression but not restart counter-reset starvation (apps/web/src/shared/hooks/__tests__/use-sse.test.ts and packages/api/src/server/router/__tests__/events.integration.test.ts). Dedupe evidence: gh issue/pr searches found related #195 and #393 but no open/closed issue covering this restart/hop live-starvation condition; open PR set contains #129 and is non-overlapping. Research links: https://orpc.dev/docs/event-iterator ; https://html.spec.whatwg.org/dev/server-sent-events.html#the-last-event-id-header
followUp: New issue: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/428. Related issues reviewed: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/195 and https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/393. Human gate remains required; do not add ready-for-dev from this automation. Suggested next trajectory for diversity: meso/observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle or micro/coding-patterns-and-local-abstractions.

title: Admin source list now defaults to owned scope
trigger: User reported that admins were seeing every user's sources in the normal sources section instead of only their own sources.
finding: The source listing use case treated admin requests without an explicit userId as global reads. That made normal source surfaces behave differently for admins than for standard users. The fix now defaults admin source lists to the current admin's own records and preserves explicit user-targeting only when a userId is supplied.
evidence: Updated packages/media/src/source/use-cases/list-sources.ts, packages/media/src/source/use-cases/__tests__/list-sources.test.ts, and packages/api/src/server/router/__tests__/source.integration.test.ts. Verified with pnpm --filter @repo/media exec vitest run src/source/use-cases/__tests__/list-sources.test.ts and pnpm --filter @repo/api exec vitest run src/server/router/__tests__/source.integration.test.ts on 2026-03-12.
followUp: Audit other normal-user list surfaces for role-based broadening so admin-only visibility changes stay inside dedicated admin workflows and not shared end-user pages.

title: Tightened podcast persona synthesis for clean names and stronger host and co-host roles
trigger: User requested that generated persona names drop prefixes like Dr and asked for a broader review of the persona generation prompt to produce better podcast host and co-host personas.
finding: Updated the persona intake prompt to steer toward audio-ready host and co-host behavior, strengthened the synthesis prompt with explicit clean-name and on-mic role rules, and added post-synthesis normalization to strip honorifics and credentials from generated speaker names.
evidence: Updated packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/chat-persona-system.ts, packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/chat-synthesize-persona-system.ts, packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/synthesize-persona.ts, packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/__tests__/persona-prompts.test.ts, and packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/__tests__/synthesize-persona.test.ts. followUp: If production outputs still drift, capture a small set of real persona creation transcripts and add regression tests around host-vs-co-host role framing and quote quality.
reflection: Prompt edits alone were too soft for the naming issue, so the safer fix was to pair the prompt change with a normalization guardrail in the synthesis use case.
feedback: When persona prompt changes affect persisted speaker labels, pair them with a deterministic cleanup step and a use-case test.
