title: Aligned persona voice selection with explicit male and female cues
trigger: User asked to make sure persona synthesis selects a voice with the correct sex for the generated name and broader persona direction.
finding: Strengthened the persona prompts to preserve explicit male/female presentation for voice selection, injected a synthesis-time guardrail when the chat clearly indicates female or male voice expectations, and added deterministic post-synthesis correction so an explicitly female persona cannot persist with a male voice or vice versa.
evidence: Updated packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/chat-persona-system.ts, packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/prompts/chat-synthesize-persona-system.ts, packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/synthesize-persona.ts, packages/ai/src/prompt-registry/__tests__/persona-prompts.test.ts, and packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/__tests__/synthesize-persona.test.ts. Validated with pnpm --filter @repo/ai test -- src/prompt-registry/__tests__/persona-prompts.test.ts src/chat/use-cases/__tests__/synthesize-persona.test.ts src/prompt-registry/__tests__/registry.test.ts and pnpm --filter @repo/ai typecheck on March 12, 2026.
followUp: If users want strict name-based voice sex enforcement beyond explicit chat cues, add a dedicated structured persona field instead of relying on a broad first-name heuristic.
reflection: The safe line was to enforce only high-confidence male/female cues from the conversation and generated honorifics; a global name-to-sex guesser would create avoidable false matches.
feedback: For persisted voice defaults, pair prompt instructions with deterministic validation whenever the model can pick from gendered voice catalogs.

title: Raised persona synthesis temperature to reduce generic outputs
trigger: User asked to increase persona generation temperature to 0.5 because the current structured outputs feel generic.
finding: Raised the primary structured persona synthesis temperature from 0.3 to 0.5 while keeping the retry path at 0.2 for schema reliability, and added test assertions so both temperatures stay pinned.
evidence: Updated packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/synthesize-persona.ts and packages/ai/src/chat/use-cases/__tests__/synthesize-persona.test.ts. Validated with pnpm --filter @repo/ai test -- src/chat/use-cases/__tests__/synthesize-persona.test.ts and pnpm --filter @repo/ai typecheck on March 12, 2026.
followUp: If personas still feel flat after this, the next lever should be prompt specificity or a slightly higher temperature on the conversational intake step, not a more aggressive fallback temperature.
reflection: The generic feel is more likely coming from the structured synthesis pass than the already-high chat temperature, so raising only the primary synthesis call is the lowest-risk change.
feedback: When tuning creativity in schema-constrained generation, change the primary path first and keep the compact retry path conservative.

title: URL scraper relies on Node fetch global dispatcher
trigger: Verify the source URL fetch path uses the same proxy/global dispatcher configured by server and worker boot.
finding: Node built-in fetch is powered by Undici, and Undici documents that the global dispatcher is shared with Node's bundled implementation. Because apps/server/src/server.ts and apps/worker/src/worker.ts call configureProxy() before other networked imports, the URL scraper's plain fetch path already uses the configured proxy/dispatcher.
evidence: Checked installed Undici docs in packages/media/node_modules/undici/README.md and the boot order in apps/server/src/server.ts plus apps/worker/src/worker.ts. Added an inline note in packages/media/src/source/services/url-scraper-impl.ts to document the invariant.
followUp: If this fetch path is ever moved to a non-Node runtime or swapped to a polyfilled fetch implementation, re-verify dispatcher/proxy inheritance and add an explicit wrapper if needed.

title: Podcast workbench planner editing now uses unified plan-first generation
trigger: Added episode planner editing to the podcast workbench settings while keeping plan-first script generation unified.
finding: The workbench can reuse the shared episode-plan generator and editor if planner drafts are treated as full-regeneration changes and source-only saves clear stale plans.
evidence: Implemented a non-persisting generatePlanDraft endpoint/use case, shared EpisodePlanEditor UI, a workbench planner draft hook, and save orchestration that persists edited plans or clears stale ones before generate.
followUp: If product later wants AI rebuilds to preview unsaved source edits, add a separate preview planner endpoint with source override authorization rather than overloading persisted-state rebuilds.

title: Podcast workbench settings moved to section tabs
trigger: The workbench settings column became too dense after adding the planner, so the settings surface was reorganized into section tabs.

title: Podcast workbench flattened top-level tabs replace generic settings tab

finding: The podcast workbench is easier to navigate when script and each editing area are peer tabs at the top level instead of hiding voice, duration, direction, planner, and sources behind a generic Settings tab.

followUp: If users bounce between script and one specific section repeatedly, persist the last active workbench tab per podcast in local UI state or URL state.

title: Podcast workbench hides duration controls and source-add affordance
trigger: The workbench still felt busy after flattening tabs, so duration editing and source-add UI were removed to narrow the available options.

followUp: When SourceList copy changes again, update shared source-guidance constants rather than duplicating marketing text inside tests.

title: Voiceover writing assistant edit confirmations now persist in chat
trigger: User reported that when the voiceover writing assistant applies a script edit, the chat should also show a visible confirmation message.
finding: The hook already tried to append an assistant confirmation, but it depended on mutating the underlying useChat message list during the same edit/follow-up cycle. Deriving confirmation bubbles from successfully applied tool-call ids is more stable and keeps the edit notice visible in the thread.
evidence: Updated apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/hooks/use-writing-assistant-chat.ts to track applied updateVoiceoverText tool calls and synthesize confirmation assistant messages from render state, updated apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/use-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts to assert the returned messages include the confirmation bubble, validated with pnpm --filter web typecheck, and ran pnpm --filter web test -- src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/use-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/writing-assistant-panel.test.tsx src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/writing-assistant-container.test.tsx where the touched voiceover tests passed but the broader web suite still has an unrelated existing failure in src/features/podcasts/__tests__/podcast-settings.test.tsx (useVoicePreviewController is not defined).
followUp: If more chat tools need local-only confirmations, centralize synthesized tool-result message derivation instead of mutating useChat state from effects.
reflection: Keeping the notification as derived UI state avoided racing the streaming chat transport.
feedback: Targeted vitest invocation in this package currently expands into the broader web suite, so validation summaries need to call out unrelated existing failures explicitly.

title: Voiceover assistant edit confirmation now survives output-available tool state
trigger: User reported with a screenshot that the voiceover writing assistant changed the script but still showed no assistant confirmation in chat.
finding: The regression was caused by the AI SDK updating tool parts from input-available to output-available after addToolResult; the confirmation synthesis only recognized input-available parts, so the edit notice disappeared immediately after the script change applied.
evidence: Updated apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/hooks/use-writing-assistant-chat.ts so confirmation synthesis accepts updateVoiceoverText parts in both input-available and output-available states while pending-write extraction stays input-available only. Expanded apps/web/src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/use-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts to simulate the real state transition after addToolResult. Validated with pnpm --filter web test -- --run src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/use-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts, pnpm --filter web exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/features/voiceovers/hooks/use-writing-assistant-chat.ts src/features/voiceovers/__tests__/use-writing-assistant-chat.test.ts, and pnpm --filter web typecheck.
followUp: When chat tools depend on SDK-managed part states, tests should simulate the full state transition instead of only the initial input-available snapshot.
reflection: Reproducing the SDK state transition in the hook test exposed the real bug faster than adding more UI-only assertions.
feedback: Focused web test commands in this package still fan out to the wider suite, so use --run and report the actual scope that Vitest executes.

title: Frontend testing docs now call out copy-locking and fragmented dialog tests
trigger: User requested the first frontend test-cleanup pass: tighten the docs, refactor the initial persona test, and define an hourly follow-up automation.
finding: The repo docs discouraged test bloat in principle but did not explicitly ban copy-only assertions, thin container wiring tests, or fragmented dialog-state suites. PersonaChatDialog also spread one small state machine across six narrow tests instead of a few scenario-driven cases.
evidence: Updated docs/frontend/testing.md and docs/testing/overview.md with explicit frontend anti-pattern rules, consolidated apps/web/src/features/personas/__tests__/persona-chat-dialog.test.tsx into three scenario tests covering manual create, pending/retry, and auto-trigger flows, and validated with pnpm --filter web test.
followUp: Use the new rules in later passes to delete or merge the remaining low-signal persona copy and wiring tests, and keep the hourly automation focused on one bounded cleanup slice per run.
reflection: Scenario-driven dialog tests kept behavioral coverage while cutting state-fragment duplication.
feedback: Pending-state buttons can expose spinner status text in their accessible name, so pending assertions should favor regex or other resilient role-based selectors.
